title: Object 9
wikitext:
{{CSS|User:SherlsF/canon.css}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Object 9: Gate Carvers}} {{Blurimage|file=Gate carver.png|caption=A Gate Carver in [[Level 96]], found as Figure 5.6 in a declassified document.}} == Description == '''Object 9''', more commonly referred to as '''Gate Carvers''', are replicable electronic devices used to create apertures in ground, roof, and wall surfaces. They are used to create temporary or permanent gateways to other levels. Their effects can be deactivated by simply patching the opening. Where they lead can be manipulated via altering their programs, which primarily use the programming language Fortran, or rarely, C. Unmodified units require continuous external power, though the ones altered by wanderers may contain internal compartments where batteries could be placed. They are extremely rare, restricted to the most powerful Backrooms settlements. Thus, their existence was only known to the top leadership of [[M.E.G.]] and allied organizations until 2015. Unlike most electronic devices in the Backrooms, Gate Carvers exhibit minimal human engineering. Traces of human contact (e.g., fingerprints, structural imperfections) were missing during its discovery in [[Level 1]]. Its appearance resembles LIDAR scanners but lacks essential components, such as scanning lenses, laser emitter indicators, and brand logos. Internally, however, it is filled with twisting, interlocking geometries, particulate inside sealed compartments, and floating cores. According to Backrooms Today and several intelligence sources hostile to M.E.G., the structural and material features were perfectly replicable using elements found in the Frontrooms and other substances within the Backrooms. The earliest documented use of a Gate Carver was in 1999. Two years later, in 2001, its inner workings were first successfully reverse-engineered by an unnamed engineer in the [[Democratic Republic of the Survivalists]]. General information tied to the device has been deliberately declassified gradually, but the underlying physical principles remain unresolved. Additionally, the rationale pertaining to the Gate Carver’s proliferation remains tightly regulated and has been the root of several tensions among organizations in the past. {{Blurimage|file=Image 2026-03-14 010052632.png|caption=A part of a BMI supercomputer initially discovered in Level 1, formerly used to manipulate Gate Carver programs.}} == Usage == Usage of Gate Carvers varies depending on the device type and configuration. In general, the process begins by carving a hole or weakening the target surface. The surface must be rigid, non-conductive, and stationary, with a consistent internal structure. As a result, this rules out most metals, fabrics, organic matter, and crystalline structures, as their magnetic properties, soft textures, or reflective characteristics render them unsuitable for gateway formation. Once the surface is prepared, the device is pointed directly toward the marked area. Although the gateway usually takes on the shape of the opening one has made, cloth or string is still commonly used to further define and control the gateway’s intended size or shape. Importantly, before activation, it is recommended that the surrounding area—approximately 12 meters from the device—be cleared of magnetic objects. This is because such objects may be rapidly attracted to the device during activation, often within milliseconds, which can potentially cause serious injuries to nearby personnel or irreversible damage to the equipment. When a brief flash of light comparable in brightness to a handheld flashlight occurs, that typically indicates a successful activation, and the gateway can be used. On the other hand, failed activations are usually accompanied by several visible streamers of light. The effects of passing through an unsuccessful gateway remain unknown. Generated gateways persist indefinitely. They only gradually disappear when one patches the opening with the original material or, more conveniently, covers it with a material like cloth or leather. Unsuccessful gateways can also be removed via this method. Restarting the process does not require that much time. == Location == The locations of Gate Carvers are classified. Nevertheless, they are known to be housed in secure laboratories within major settlements operated by M.E.G., the [[Backrooms Colonists]], and the [[Democratic Republic of the Survivalists]]. However, damaged or dismantled units have been reported on certain levels—such as Level 1 and [[Level 20]]—presumably discarded after becoming nonfunctional. {{Blurimage|file=Image 2026-03-14 010632412.png|caption=A typical Stabilizer whose handle is colored red. Cropped from previous image.}} == Additional Information == === Stabilizers === Stabilizers are man-made devices that can be connected via copper cables to modified Gate Carvers. The Stabilizers monitor the local spacetime metric and maintain the environment of the gateway by sustaining a region of negative energy density via vacuum cavities and the Casimir Effect. The active gateways show minor oscillations as the two regions tend to separate. The Stabilizer sends pulses that maintain the phase between both ends of the aperture. This maintains a stable environment and keeps the regions from shearing or collapsing in the wormhole metric as defined by General Relativity. The Stabilizer will reduce the energy input if the instability is greater than a certain threshold. This causes a controlled shutdown rather than a catastrophic failure in the metric. In addition, this component is the only part of the Gate Carver system that is regarded as optional, as gateways that are generated by Gate Carvers are already considered safe through multiple experiments. Stabilizers’ main usage is to sustain botched gateways long enough in case of an emergency. It is also the only part for which information is available regarding its internal workings. ----
